g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is R3M and why is it used for wrist camera input?')
sendPrompt('How does the CNN encoder process tactile images?')
sendPrompt('What is the forcefield input and how is it encoded?')
sendPrompt('What does the robot state vector contain?')
sendPrompt('How does the input fusion work before the IQL heads?')
sendPrompt('Why does each IQL head have its own encoder copy?')
sendPrompt('Why does each IQL head have its own encoder copy?')
sendPrompt('Why does each IQL head have its own encoder copy?')
sendPrompt('What does the value head V(s) do in IQL?')
sendPrompt('What does the double critic Q(s,a) do in IQL?')
sendPrompt('What does the actor head do in IQL?')

svg.text: Separate encoder copies = separate gradient flows
Actor loss won't corrupt critic's learned features
Value loss: V(s) ← expectile regression on Q
Critic loss: Q(s,a) ← Bellman backup using V
Actor loss: log π(a|s) weighted by advantage
Each loss trains its own encoder end-to-end
